title: True race-lap query ranking model, not just rank blending
summary: The strongest current solutions only rank-blend binary classifiers, but CatBoost and LightGBM support query/group ranking objectives directly.
rationale: The strongest current solutions only rank-blend binary classifiers, but CatBoost and LightGBM support query/group ranking objectives directly. This problem is naturally grouped by `Year/Race/LapNumber`: on each lap, some drivers are more stop-imminent than others. Adjacent competitions often gain when a ranking model is added as a decorrelated member instead of forcing all signal through logloss.
implementation_hint: Build queries as all cars on the same `Year/Race/LapNumber`. Start with binary relevance `PitNextLap`; if too sparse, try soft relevance such as inverse laps-until-next-stop clipped to a short horizon. Train CatBoost `QueryCrossEntropy` or `YetiRank` with group ids, then blend its OOF ranks with the existing XGB/Cat classifiers. Evaluate only with race-level grouped splits plus a forward gap because neighboring laps are highly correlated.
expected_effect: Can improve cross-sectional ordering within each lap, which is exactly where ROC AUC gains can still come from after feature engineering saturates.
risk: Ranking loss may learn only within-lap order and ignore absolute calibration across laps; it should be an ensemble member, not an outright replacement.
